title: kubectl Discovery, Contexts, Queries, Diff, Apply, and Safe Mutation · Changed-Cluster Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer kubectl discovery, contexts, queries, diff, apply, and safe mutation into a new safe kubectl investigation and apply record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of safe kubectl investigation and apply record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Trace kubeconfig loading, merged files, context, cluster, user, namespace, credentials, impersonation, and TLS server identity before a request.
Use API discovery, api-resources, api-versions, explain, raw endpoints, and versioned reference material to identify authoritative fields.
Choose get, describe, events, logs, JSONPath, custom columns, selectors, and raw JSON or YAML without parsing human tables as stable interfaces.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Kubeconfig Context Authority
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab operator is pointed at production instead of training, relies on table output, runs client dry-run, and plans an unscoped prune. Make context, namespace, server validation, field ownership, diff, and UID-safe mutation explicit. Competency probe: Trace kubeconfig loading, merged files, context, cluster, user, namespace, credentials, impersonation, and TLS server identity before a request.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Kubeconfig Context Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace kubeconfig loading, merged files, context, cluster, user, namespace, credentials, impersonation, and tls server identity before a request. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Kubeconfig Context Authority, keep K8s Kubeconfig Context Authority connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety-jkli4a". Tailor its kubectl-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety, keep K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety-jkli4a
  - competency: k8s-imperative-dry-run-safety
    artifact: kubectl-plan.yaml
    decision: imperative dry run safety
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: jkli4a
    change: |
      context: training-cluster
      namespace: intake
      manager: learn-it-all
      preview: server-dry-run-and-diff
      mutation: server-side-apply
      delete: uid-precondition
    verify: kubectl config current-context && kubectl diff --server-side -f release.yaml
    failure: Reject implicit context, human-table parsing, client dry run as admission proof, unscoped prune, or field ownership theft.
    repair: Name cluster and namespace, use discovery and structured output, preview server behavior, scope ownership, and verify changed status.

content.value: Kubernetes Architecture, Objects, and Cluster Administration — Controls control-plane and node components, API and etcd boundaries, reconciliation, object identity and status, namespaces, ownership, cluster lifecycle, conformance, and recovery.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab K8s Imperative Dry Run Safety
